verdict.answer_first: Both answer in sentence 1: A says "A closure is a function that remembers"; B says "A closure is a function that remembers".
verdict.decisiveness: B commits cleanly to one practical example and takeaway; A adds extra sections and ends by handing off with "Want me to show one of these".
verdict.fabrication: A mislabels the example: "Practical example: a rate limiter" but then shows a "once helper"; B: none.
verdict.actionability: B is easier to act on because the account example shows a complete use case with controlled methods: "deposit", "withdraw", "getBalance".
